commit.name: Weather Card
commit.description: Data display with props, design tokens, and responsive layout
commit.prompt:
Build a weather card component. It should display:
- Current temperature (large, prominent)
- Weather condition with an appropriate icon/emoji (sunny, cloudy, rainy, etc.)
- Humidity percentage
- Wind speed
- A 5-day forecast strip at the bottom showing day name, icon, and high/low temps

All data must come from props — never hardcode weather data. Use CSS variables from the design system for all colors and spacing.

evaluation.critique: The component has good layout structure with proper use of Stack/Row and design tokens, but has several issues: nested Containers are redundant, the forecast strip lacks responsive wrapping for mobile, and missing interactive polish (hover states, transitions). Data presentation could be improved by handling undefined forecast values and validating wind speed units.

commit.name: Multi-Step Survey Form
commit.description: Multi-step form with validation, progress tracking, and review
commit.prompt:
Build a multi-step survey form with 4 steps:
- Step 1: Text inputs for name (required) and email (required, must be valid email format) with inline validation
- Step 2: Radio button group for satisfaction rating (1-5 scale with labels from props)
- Step 3: Checkbox group for feature interests (options from props)
- Step 4: Textarea for open-ended comments (optional, with character counter, max 500 chars)

Requirements:
- Progress bar at the top showing current step (e.g., "Step 2 of 4")
- Back/Next navigation buttons (Next disabled until required fields valid)
- Final review step showing all answers before submission
- Call props.onSubmit with all collected answers as a structured object
- All option labels and feature lists must come from props
- Use design system CSS variables for all styling

featureOptions.description: Available feature options for the checkbox step

satisfactionLabels.description: Labels for satisfaction rating (e.g., Very Unsatisfied to Very Satisfied)

submit.label: Submit Survey
submit.description: Called with all collected answers as a structured object

commit.name: Kanban Task Board
commit.description: Column-based task board with task cards and state management
commit.prompt:
Build a Kanban-style task board with 3 columns: To Do, In Progress, Done.

Each column:
- Has a header with column name and task count
- Contains a vertical list of task cards
- Has an "Add Task" button at the bottom

Each task card displays:
- Task title (editable on double-click or via edit button)
- Priority badge (low/medium/high with color coding from design tokens)
- Assignee avatar or initials
- Due date (if set)
- A move button or dropdown to move the task to another column

Requirements:
- Tasks can be moved between columns via move controls (buttons or select dropdown)
- New tasks start in "To Do" column
- "Add Task" shows an inline form with title input and priority selector
- Track total tasks and completed count in a summary bar at the top
- Initial tasks come from props; real-time updates from other team members arrive via stream
- Call the taskUpdate action when a task is moved, edited, or created
- Use design system CSS variables for all colors, spacing, and shadows

tasks.description: Array of tasks with id, title, priority (low/medium/high), assignee initials, column ID, and optional dueDate

columns.description: Column definitions: [{id: "todo", name: "To Do"}, {id: "in-progress", name: "In Progress"}, {id: "done", name: "Done"}]

taskUpdate.label: Task Updated
taskUpdate.description: Called when a task is moved, edited, or created. Payload: { action, taskId, data }

stream.description: Real-time task updates from other team members

taskChanged.description: A task was moved, edited, or created by another user — update it in the board

getBoardState.description: Current board state including any unsaved edits

evaluation.critique: The Kanban board has a solid layout with proper flex columns and good use of design tokens (spacing, semantic colors), but lacks visual polish with missing hover/focus states, incomplete task card information (assignee avatars/initials and due dates aren't rendered), and the code appears truncated mid-component. The hierarchy is clear but could benefit from better visual distinction between columns and task priorities.

commit.name: Interactive Periodic Table
commit.description: CSS Grid periodic table with category coloring, search, and detail panel
commit.prompt:
Build an interactive periodic table of elements.

Layout:
- CSS Grid: 18 columns. Place each element using gridRow and gridColumn from its data.
- Each cell: symbol (large, centered), atomic number (small, top-left)
- Color-code by category using design tokens (e.g., noble-gas → one color, halogen → another)
- Search input at top — filters by name or symbol (case-insensitive)
- Click an element → detail panel below grid shows: name, symbol, atomicNumber, atomicWeight, category
- Legend showing each category with its color

All element data comes from props.elements. Use design system CSS variables.

issues.description: The search input is implemented using the `Input` primitive, but the requirement specifically requested a search input (which typically implies an icon or specialized behavior for search). More importantly, the Search functionality only filters the visible table but does not clarify how non-matching elements should be handled (they currently disappear entirely from the grid layout, which breaks the physical structure of a periodic table).
issues.fix: The search input is implemented using the `Input` primitive, but the requirement specifically requested a search input (which typically implies an icon or specialized behavior for search). More importantly, the Search functionality only filters the visible table but does not clarify how non-matching elements should be handled (they currently disappear entirely from the grid layout, which breaks the physical structure of a periodic table).

issues.description: The legend color-coding is implemented, but the category mapping in `CATEGORIES` (lines 28-38) is incomplete and does not match the actual categories provided in the data contract (e.g., 'noble-gas' vs 'Noble gas'). As a result, elements will default to the 'surfaceVariant' color rather than their correct category color.

commit.name: E-Commerce Product Page
commit.description: Product detail page with image, tabs, quantity selector, and cart
commit.prompt:
Build an e-commerce product detail page. Layout:

Top section:
- Product image placeholder (use a colored box with the product name)
- Product title, price (with currency from props), and rating stars (1-5)
- Stock status badge ("In Stock" green or "Low Stock" warning)

Middle section — Tab navigation with 3 tabs:
- "Description" tab: renders product description text from props
- "Specifications" tab: renders key-value pairs from props as a table
- "Reviews" tab: renders a list of reviews (author, rating, text, date) from props

Bottom section:
- Quantity selector (increment/decrement buttons, min 1, max from stock)
- "Add to Cart" button (disabled if out of stock)
- Call props.onAddToCart with { productId, quantity } when clicked

Requirements:
- All product data from props — nothing hardcoded
- Use design system tokens for all colors, spacing, typography
- Price should format with currency symbol from props
- Rating stars should be visual (filled/empty)
- Responsive: stack image above details on mobile widths

product.description: Product object with id, name, price, currency, rating, stock, description, specifications[], reviews[]

example.name: Wireless Noise-Cancelling Headphones
example.description: Premium wireless headphones with adaptive noise cancellation, 30-hour battery life, and ultra-comfortable memory foam ear cushions.

reviews.author: Alex M.
reviews.text: Best headphones I have ever owned. The noise cancellation is incredible.

addToCart.label: Add to Cart
addToCart.description: Called when user clicks Add to Cart. Payload: { productId, quantity }

product.name: Wireless Noise-Cancelling Headphones
product.description: Premium wireless headphones with adaptive noise cancellation, 30-hour battery life, and ultra-comfortable memory foam ear cushions.

reviews.author: Alex M.
reviews.text: Best headphones I have ever owned. The noise cancellation is incredible.

reviews.author: Sarah K.
reviews.text: Great sound quality. A bit tight for my head but breaks in after a week.

reviews.author: James P.
reviews.text: Battery life is exactly as advertised. Very impressed.

The polish is good with proper disabled states and interactions, though the quantity controls could benefit from input validation feedback.

commit.name: Chat Interface
commit.description: Chat UI with message list, input area, and send callback
commit.prompt:
Build a chat interface component with real-time messaging. Layout:

Header:
- Chat title from props
- Participant count badge
- Typing indicator (shows when another user is typing, received via stream)

Message list:
- Scrollable area showing messages (initial from props, new ones from stream)
- Each message shows: sender name, message text, formatted timestamp
- Messages from the current user (where sender matches props.currentUser) align right with primary color background
- Messages from others align left with surfaceVariant background
- Group consecutive messages from same sender

Input area at the bottom:
- Text input field for composing messages
- Send button (disabled when input is empty)
- Call the sendMessage action with { text, timestamp: new Date().toISOString() } on click or Enter

Requirements:
- Initial messages from props, new messages arrive via stream — append them to the list
- Typing indicator appears/disappears via stream events
- Use design system CSS variables for all colors and spacing
- Auto-scroll to bottom when new messages arrive

currentUser.description: Current user name (for right-aligning own messages)

messages.description: Initial messages to display on mount

example.text: Hey team, the design review is at 3pm

sendMessage.label: Send Message
sendMessage.description: Called when user sends a message. Payload: { text, timestamp }

stream.description: Real-time chat events from the agent

message.description: New message from another participant — append to message list

typing.description: Typing indicator — show/hide based on active flag

loadHistory.label: Load History
loadHistory.description: Fetch older messages for infinite scroll

getDraftState.description: Current message draft and typing state

messages.text: Hey team, the design review is at 3pm
Got it, I will prepare the mockups
Also, can someone review the color palette?
I pushed the latest changes to the design branch
I will take a look after the meeting

evaluation.critique: The component has a solid foundation with proper use of design tokens and correct data rendering from props/streams. Main issues: the message container uses inline style for maxHeight/overflowY instead of design tokens, missing scroll-to-bottom behavior for new messages, and the Send button lacks hover/focus polish. The duplicate export statement is a syntax error that needs fixing.

commit.name: Stock Ticker Dashboard
commit.description: Stock price display with color-coded changes and responsive grid
commit.prompt:
Build a stock ticker dashboard that displays stock prices with live updates. Layout:
- Header with "Market Watch" title and a "Last Updated" timestamp (updates in real-time)
- Responsive grid of stock cards (3 per row on desktop, 1 on mobile)
- Each card shows: ticker symbol (large, bold), company name, current price formatted with 2 decimals, change amount, and change percentage
- Color coding: green text/border for positive change, red for negative change
- A subtle arrow icon (▲ or ▼) next to the change percentage
- Brief flash/highlight animation when a stock price updates

Initial stock data comes from props. Live price updates arrive via stream events — merge them into the displayed data. Use design system CSS variables for all colors and spacing.

stocks.description: Array of stocks with symbol, name, price, change, changePercent

stream.description: Real-time stock price updates from the agent

priceUpdate.description: Updated price data for one or more stocks

getStockDetail.label: Stock Detail
getStockDetail.description: Get detailed info for a specific stock (52-week range, volume, etc.)

evaluation.critique: The component has a syntax error (duplicate return statement) that would break compilation. While it uses design tokens in many places, there's inconsistent usage (hardcoded `gap={16}` in CardGrid instead of spacing tokens) and the animation implementation references non-existent token properties. The hierarchy and data presentation are generally solid with good real-time updates and formatting.

commit.name: Onboarding Wizard
commit.description: Multi-step onboarding flow with step indicator and validation
commit.prompt:
Build a 3-step onboarding wizard. Steps:

Step 1 - Profile Setup:
- Name input (required)
- Email input (required, validate email format)
- Avatar selection (choose from 4 emoji avatars)

Step 2 - Preferences:
- Role selection (dropdown: Developer, Designer, Manager, Other)
- Notification toggle (email notifications on/off)
- Theme preference (light/dark toggle)

Step 3 - Review & Confirm:
- Summary of all selections from steps 1-2
- "Go Back" button to edit
- "Complete Setup" button to submit

Requirements:
- Step indicator at top showing current step (1/2/3) with progress bar
- Back/Next navigation (Next disabled until required fields valid)
- Smooth transition between steps
- Call props.onComplete with all collected data on final submit
- All initial values from props (for editing existing profile)
- Use design system CSS variables for all styling

initialProfile.description: Pre-filled profile data for editing (all fields optional)

avatarOptions.description: Available avatar emojis to choose from

roleOptions.description: Available role options for the dropdown

complete.label: Complete Setup
complete.description: Called with all profile data when user completes onboarding
